title: Wano Prepares While the World Shakes
description: On pages 6–9, this part focuses on Luffy, Unknown person #5, Kinemon, Unknown person #6, Queen, Unknown person #7 as The raid preparations accelerate while outside Wano, Reverie news shakes the world.
scene: The raid preparations accelerate while outside Wano, Reverie news shakes the world.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Remember how completely useless brute force was against him!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There's no time to waste! Your enemy is a monster!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He's beating that steel plate like it's made of jelly...

dialogue.speaker: Kinemon
dialogue.text: Five days left until the Onigashima raid.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I would like you make a small change to the picture puzzles you all have in hand.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Add two lines to the belly of the viper. That is Lord Yasuie's message!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Kuri Itachi Harbor ship repair squad

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Two lines... I see! I get it now!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: All these ships up! Super leave it to me!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I'd expect nothing less of Lord Yasuie!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wow! I guess it really does make immediate sense to people from Wano.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So, what does it mean? Two lines to Habu makes it Babu?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: is quite difficult to understand unless one knows the names of the harbors in Wano.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The viper in the original picture signified Habu Harbor.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This new version adds two lines to it.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Each of Wano's Harbors sounds like the name of an animal.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And although it looks a little long, adding legs to a viper makes it look sort of like a lizard.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: which signifies toage harbor, in upon.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Changing our meeting place to this harbor nearly guarantees

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: that our raid won't be stopped by our enemies.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Lord Yasuie considered all the options available to him and made a game time decision.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Till the very end, we've relied on the help and mercy of our saviors.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If we are unable to repay them, it will be an unforgettable shame!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: After all, Lord Yasuie did tell us once...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We are currently bolstering our ranks at a truly ideal rate.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We have not yet managed to gather any information on denjiro,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But we have managed to gather seven of Lord Oden's retainers!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: I can't believe it. Our goal

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: of 5000 soldiers isn't even a dream anymore.

summary: This section advances the Wano - Before the Raid and World News storyline with 59 dialogue beats. The raid preparations accelerate while outside Wano, Reverie news shakes the world.
